ActionsMap.description: An actions assertion provides information on edits and other actions taken that affect the asset's content. There will be an array of actions - each action declaring _what_ took place on the asset and (optionally) _when_ it took place, along with possible other information such as what software performed the action. Except where noted in , the order of actions in this array is unspecified, and does not imply the order in which actions were performed.

This assertion allows for specifying an IANA Media Type value and/or additional type information, since many assets have formats that cannot be completely described by a single Media Type value.

properties.name: exclusions
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute this hash, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken from an enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hashes in this Merkle tree, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken from the `alg` value of the enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: hash
properties.description: For non-fragmented MP4, this is the hash of the entire BMFF file excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For fragmented MP4 (live or VOD), this field is the hash of the segment excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.

properties.name: merkle
properties.description: A set of Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable verification of a single 'mdat' box, multiple 'mdat' boxes, and/or individual fragment files within the asset.

properties.name: name
properties.description: optional) a human-readable description of what this hash covers.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Unused and deprecated.

properties.name: sequenceNumber
properties.description: in a live video workflow, the monotonically increasing sequence number for each segment

properties.name: xpath
properties.description: Location of box(es) to exclude from the hash starting from the root node as an xpath formatted string of version https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-10/ with highly constrained syntax.

properties.name: length
properties.description: (optional) Length that a leafmost box must have to exclude from the hash.

properties.name: data
properties.description: (optional) The data in the leafmost box at the specified relative byte offset must be identical to the specified data for the box to be excluded from the hash.

properties.name: subset
properties.description: (optional) Only this portion of the excluded box is excluded from the hash.  Each entry in the array must have a monotonically increasing relative byte offset.  No subset within the array may overlap.  The last entry may have a length of zero; this indicates that the remainder of the box from that relative byte offset onward is excluded.  A relative byte offset or relative byte offset plus length that exceeds the length of the box is allowed; bytes beyond the end of the box are never hashed.

properties.name: version
properties.description: (optional) Version that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Only specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

properties.name: flags
properties.description: (optional) byte string of exactly 3 bytes.  The 24-bit flags that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Only specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

properties.name: exact
properties.description: (optional) indicates that flags must be an exact match.  If not specified, defaults to true.  Only specified for a box that inherits from FullBox and when flags is also specified.

relationships.name: flags
relationships.description: (optional) byte string of exactly 3 bytes.  The 24-bit flags that must be set in a leafmost box for the box to be excluded from the hash.  Only specified for a box that inherits from FullBox.

MerkleMap.name: MerkleMap

properties.name: localId
properties.description: A local id indicating Merkle tree.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Number of leaf nodes in the Merkle tree.  Null nodes are not included in this count.

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hashes in this Merkle tree, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken from the `alg` value of the enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: initHash
properties.description: For fragmented MP4 assets that are split across multiple files, this field is required to be present and is the hash of the entire initialization segment file for chunks hashed by this Merkle tree excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For fragmented MP4 assets that are stored as a single flat MP4 file, this field is required to be present and is the hash of all bytes preceding the first 'moof' box excluding boxes listed in the exclusions array.  For non-fragmented MP4, this field is required to be absent.

properties.name: hashes
properties.description: An ordered array representing a single row of the Merkle tree, which may be the leaf-most row, root row, or any intermediate row. Any null nodes are excluded. The depth of the row is implied by (is computed from) the number of items in this array.

properties.name: fixedBlockSize
properties.description: For non-fragmented MP4 assets where the mdat box is validated piecewise, this field can be present. This field is the non-negative size in bytes of a given leaf node in the Merkle tree. For fragmented MP4, this field is not present.

properties.name: variableBlockSizes
properties.description: For non-fragmented MP4 assets where the mdat box is validated piecewise, this field can be present. Each entry in the array corresponds to the non-negative size in bytes of a given leaf node in the Merkle tree. The number of elements is equal to `count` and sum of the values is equal to size of payload of mdat. For fragmented MP4, this field is not present.

MerkleMap.description: Each entry in a map is a Merkle tree row and the associated data required to enable validation of a single 'mdat' box or multiple 'mdat' boxes within the asset.",

BmffMerkleMap.name: BmffMerkleMap

properties.name: uniqueId
properties.description: 1-based unique id to determine which Merkle tree validates a given mdat box

properties.name: localId
properties.description: A local id indicating Merkle tree.

properties.name: location
properties.description: Zero-based index into the leaf-most Merkle tree row corresponding to this 'mdat' box or portion of this 'mdat' box

properties.name: hashes
properties.description: An ordered array representing the set of additional hashes required to reach a hash in the Merkle tree specified in the manifest from leaf-most (peer of this node) to root-most (child of node in manifest).  Note that this array may not be present, e.g. if the manifest itself contains the leaf-most row of the Merkle tree.  Null hashes are not included in this array.  The algorithm used is determined using the `alg` field from the corresponding entry in the `merkle` field array in the BMFF hash structure.

BmffMerkleMap.description: The data structure used to store sufficient information to validate a single 'mdat' box or a portion of an 'mdat' box when a Merkle tree is used",

If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken the `alg` value of the enclosing structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

properties.name: alg
properties.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hash in this assertion, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken the `alg` value of the enclosing structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. relationships.name: exclusions
relationships.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute this hash, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken from an enclosing structure as defined by that structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

relationships.name: merkle
relationships.description: A set of Merkle tree rows and the associated data required to enable verification of a single 'mdat' box, multiple 'mdat' boxes, and/or individual fragment files within the asset.

relationships.name: data
relationships.description: (optional) The data in the leafmost box at the specified relative byte offset must be identical to the specified data for the box to be excluded from the hash.

relationships.name: subset
relationships.description: (optional) Only this portion of the excluded box is excluded from the hash.  Each entry in the array must have a monotonically increasing relative byte offset.  No subset within the array may overlap.  The last entry may have a length of zero; this indicates that the remainder of the box from that relative byte offset onward is excluded.  A relative byte offset or relative byte offset plus length that exceeds the length of the box is allowed; bytes beyond the end of the box are never hashed.

relationships.name: boxes
relationships.description: A string identifying the cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute the hash in this assertion, taken from the C2PA hash algorithm identifier list. If this field is absent, the hash algorithm is taken the `alg` value of the enclosing structure. If both are present, the field in this structure is used. If no value is present in any of these places, this structure is invalid; there is no default.

validation_rules.description: As described in , a C2PA Manifest may be considered as Well-Formed, Valid or Trusted based on the results of these steps.
validation_rules.source_text: As described in , a C2PA Manifest may be considered as Well-Formed, Valid or Trusted based on the results of these steps.

validation_rules.description: Custom codes shall conform to the same syntax as entity-specific namespaces, e.g. com.litware.
validation_rules.source_text: Custom codes shall conform to the same syntax as entity-specific namespaces, e.g. com.litware.

validation_rules.description: When a claim generator adds an ingredient asset via an ingredient assertion, it shall act as a validator, and perform the full validation algorithm described in this section on the ingredient.
validation_rules.source_text: When a claim generator adds an ingredient asset via an ingredient assertion, it shall act as a validator, and perform the full validation algorithm described in this section on the ingredient.

validation_rules.description: The claim generator shall record the validation results of the ingredient, per the following  schema, as the value of the validationResults field in the ingredient assertion.
validation_rules.source_text: The claim generator shall record the validation results of the ingredient, per the following  schema, as the value of the validationResults field in the ingredient assertion.

validation_rules.description: The validation results may also contain a SemVer formatted specVersion field which represents the version of this specification that was used as the basis for the validation procedure.
validation_rules.source_text: The validation results may also contain a SemVer formatted specVersion field which represents the version of this specification that was used as the basis for the validation procedure.

validation_rules.description: It may also contain a trustListURI field, which is a URI that identifies the trust list that was used to validate the claim signature.
validation_rules.source_text: It may also contain a trustListURI field, which is a URI that identifies the trust list that was used to validate the claim signature.

validation_rules.description:
* a warning about the lack of validity, 
* a warning that the data shall not be attributed to the manifest's signer, and in the case of an ingredient manifest, additional not to the asset's manifest's signer.
validation_rules.source_text:

* a warning that the data shall not be attributed to the manifest's signer, and in the case of an ingredient manifest, additional not to the asset's manifest's

validation_rules.description: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores embedded in an asset, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.
validation_rules.source_text: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores embedded in an asset, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.

validation_rules.description: In the case where this asset is being added as an ingredient, none of these embedded C2PA Manifests shall be included in the ingredient assertion.
validation_rules.source_text: In the case where this asset is being added as an ingredient, none of these embedded C2PA Manifests shall be included in the ingredient assertion.

validation_rules.description: If an asset has both an embedded C2PA Manifest Store and a Link header indicating the presence of a remote C2PA Manifest Store, the embedded C2PA Manifest Store shall be considered the active manifest and the remote C2PA Manifest Store shall be ignored.
validation_rules.source_text: If an asset has both an embedded C2PA Manifest Store and a Link header indicating the presence of a remote C2PA Manifest Store, the embedded C2PA Manifest Store shall be considered the active manifest

validation_rules.description: This requires that PDF files support multiple C2PA Manifest Stores - though there shall only be one per update section.
validation_rules.source_text: This requires that PDF files support multiple C2PA Manifest Stores - though there shall only be one per update section.

validation_rules.description: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores present in a single update section, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.
validation_rules.source_text: If there are multiple C2PA Manifest Stores present in a single update section, they shall all be considered as invalid and the validation should treat this as if no manifests were located.

validation_rules.description: However, any C2PA Manifest Stores present in early updates of the PDF or of the original PDF, shall still be considered valid and processed accordingly.
validation_rules.source_text: However, any C2PA Manifest Stores present in early updates of the PDF or of the original PDF, shall still be considered valid and processed accordingly.

validation_rules.description: If there is no embedded C2PA Manifest Store, the following attempts should be made to locate one at a remote location.
validation_rules.source_text: If there is no embedded C2PA Manifest Store, the following attempts should be made to locate one at a remote location.

validation_rules.description: Once a C2PA Manifest Store is located, the validation process should proceed with that manifest as the active manifest (and therefore not continue looking for additional manifests).
validation_rules.source_text: Once a C2PA Manifest Store is located, the validation process should proceed with that manifest as the active manifest (and therefore not continue looking for additional manifests).

validation_rules.description: The following attempts should be made in order:
validation_rules.source_text: The following attempts should be made in order:

validation_rules.description: - If the asset has any XMP in the standard asset locations (i.e., outside the C2PA Manifest) and that XMP contains a dcterms:provenance key, the provided URI should be used to locate the active manifest.
validation_rules.source_text: - If the asset has any XMP in the standard asset locations (i.e., outside the C2PA Manifest) and that XMP contains a dcterms:provenance key, the provided URI should be used to locate the active manife

validation_rules.description: - If the asset is a font with a C2PA table and its activeManifestUriLength is non-zero, then the indicated URI should be used to locate the active manifest.
validation_rules.source_text: - If the asset is a font with a C2PA table and its activeManifestUriLength is non-zero, then the indicated URI should be used to locate the active manifest.

validation_rules.description: - If no C2PA Manifest Store has been located, the validator should look for files at the same path or URI, but with a filename extension of .c2pa.

constraints: Never use in informational, security, or threat analysis sections
Same concept uses the same normative verb throughout the spec

rules.meaning: Recommended but exceptions exist with clear justification.
constraints: Do not use 'it is recommended that' -- use 'should' directly
Verify it does not mask a 'should'
Network access is always optional -- any retrieval uses 'may attempt'

rules.contains: Mechanism description, data structures, CDDL schemas
rules.must_not_contain: Validation logic, security analysis, implementation guidance

rules.contains: How to validate the mechanism, status codes, error conditions
Security analysis, threat model, risk assessment

structural_placement.principle: The feature section defines the mechanism. Validation and security sections define how to check it. Guidance does not belong in normative spec text.

cddl_conventions.naming: kebab-case for all CDDL rule names (e.g. region-map, action-items-map-v2)

extensibility.forward_compat: Include '* tstr => any' wildcard in maps for forward compatibility

field_rules: CBOR diagnostic examples must match schema field names, types, and structure
Examples demonstrate the minimal valid case unless showing optional fields

new_assertion_checklist: Define data structure in feature section with CDDL schema
Add assertion label to the assertion label registry
Add validation rules to Validation.adoc specific assertion validation list
Add all status codes to consolidated status code tables (success/informational/failure)
Add security analysis to Threats_Harms.adoc if applicable
Include CBOR diagnostic example matching the schema
Verify extensibility: '* tstr => any' wildcard present

semantic_terms.manifest: The C2PA Manifest -- a signed collection of assertions about an asset
semantic_terms.manifest_store: The container holding one or more manifests
semantic_terms.active_manifest: The current (most recent) manifest in a manifest chain
semantic_terms.claim_generator: The entity creating the manifest, not a software component
semantic_terms.validator: The entity performing validation of a manifest
semantic_terms.trust: Claim signature verification against a trust list -- distinct from integrity
semantic_terms.integrity: Hash verification of content bindings -- distinct from trust
semantic_terms.componentOf: Parent-to-child relationship ONLY. Never describe as child-to-parent.
semantic_terms.parentOf: The derived-from/predecessor relationship in Update Manifests

cross_reference_rules: Every new assertion must appear in the Specific Assertion Validation list
Every new status code must appear in consolidated status code tables
hashed-uri references must use $hashed-uri-map in CDDL
Update Manifests have exactly one parentOf ingredient and no hard bindings
Network access is ALWAYS optional -- retrieval must use 'may attempt'
